message:
feat: add netpoller for goroutine-like async I/O

Implements Go's netpoller pattern for non-blocking I/O:
- kqueue support for macOS/BSD
- epoll support for Linux
- Integration with work-stealing scheduler
- Green threads park on I/O, wake when ready

This enables true goroutine-like behavior:
1. Green thread registers socket with netpoller
2. Thread yields (parks) and scheduler runs other work
3. OS handles I/O in background via kqueue/epoll
4. When I/O completes, netpoller wakes the thread
5. Scheduler picks up ready thread and continues

Key differences from Go:
- No preemption yet (cooperative only)
- Fixed 4KB stacks (Go uses growable stacks)
- No netpoller integration with HTTP client yet
